items.headline: Seeds of Peace Monument agreement approved for Guadalupe River Park
items.summary: City Council approved an agreement with The Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust for a Seeds of Peace Monument artwork by Mario Chiodo, to be located at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. This marks a public art addition to a key city park along the Guadalupe River.

items.headline: Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District budget set for 2026-2027
items.summary: City Council took action on the Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District's 2026-2027 budget and annual assessment. This affects property owners and businesses in that commercial corridor.

items.headline: Cupertino updates Health and Safety element of General Plan
items.summary: The City Council is updating the Health and Safety Element of the Cupertino General Plan (2015–2040 Community Vision), which addresses how the city manages public health, emergency preparedness, and safety risks. This update shapes long-term policies affecting where development occurs and how the city responds to hazards.

items.headline: City Council appoints new permanent City Manager
items.summary: The Milpitas City Council adopted a resolution on May 19 appointing a permanent City Manager. This follows what was likely an interim or acting arrangement and represents a significant leadership decision for the city's administrative operations.

items.headline: New duplex permitted on Old Piedmont Road in San José
items.summary: A permit has been issued for a new two-story, two-unit duplex at 2174 Old Piedmont Road, valued at over $1 million. This represents one of the few new multi-unit residential projects in this batch of permits.

items.headline: City releases draft water shortage plan for public review
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission reviewed Sunnyvale's Draft 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. The contingency plan outlines how the city will manage water supply during shortages, a critical planning document as California faces ongoing drought pressures.

items.headline: City Council to hear residential electrification program proposals
items.summary: The City Council will receive a status update on residential electrification and consider proposed principles for programs to help Palo Alto comply with regional air quality regulations. This addresses the shift away from natural gas heating and appliances in homes.

items.headline: Council to amend zoning rules for accessory dwelling units
items.summary: Palo Alto is updating its municipal code to allow more accessory and junior accessory dwelling units in response to changes in state law. This could increase housing options on single-family residential lots.
